name: Bartlett v. Strickland
question: Is a North Carolina redistricting plan in a county lacking a numerical majority of minority citizens required by the Voting Rights of Act of 1965 and, therefore, exempt from complying with the conditions of the North Carolina Constitution.
description: A case in which the Court found that the Voting Rights Act of 1965 did not require redistricting in a county lacking a numerical majority of a racial minority of citizens.
